summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on October 10, 2023. Key discussions included a CPAAA memorial Christmas tree fundraiser proposal that needs legal review, a zoning text amendment for temporary dwelling units during new home construction that was voted down for rewriting to also address emergency/renovation situations, and planning for the 2024 OTA winter conference. The board also went into executive session regarding a personnel matter and adjourned with no decisions made from that session.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (Sept 26 – Oct 10, 2023) – passed
Resolution R-021-2023 for zoning text amendment on temporary dwelling units during new home construction – voted NO (denied for rewriting to include emergency/renovation provisions)
Motion to approve revised township resources in support of Goshen Chamber of Commerce Light Up Goshen Parade on 11/18 – passed
Motion to approve three days unpaid leave for Inspector Hundley (12/26–12/28/2023) – passed
Motion to approve revised authorized credit card users list (added Melinda, Donna, Joe Reynolds; removed former employee) – passed
Motion to approve attendance at OTA winter conference (February 2024) for Laura Englund, Chad Meadows, Donna Ross, Bob Rose, Jen Farmer, and any board members – passed
Motion to increase revenue line 1-293-599-0000 for $33,565 (airbag refund reimbursement) – passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session under ORC 121.22(G)(1) at 6:34 PM – passed
Motion to adjourn regular meeting at 6:52 PM – passed
action_items: Administrator Rose to check with prosecutor's office on legality of using public property for CPAAA memorial tree fundraiser

Liz/Zoning to rewrite zoning text amendment to add a separate provision (item 8) for temporary dwelling units during major renovation or emergency situations, and bring revised version to next meeting
Chief to work with Rose on CPAAA fundraiser legal review
Michelle to find out the species/type of the balled Christmas trees being offered
Administrator Rose to coordinate hotel arrangements and logistics for OTA winter conference in February 2024
Sign placard to be updated on Route 28 sign this week or next
Chief Myers to send CFO recertification certificate
key_amounts: $48,000 – body worn camera grant application amount
$33,565 – revenue increase for lifting airbag reimbursement from 2018
$100 – past cost per memorial tree for CPAAA fundraiser
$2.5 million – amount requested from state legislators (House Bill 244)
$200 million – investment in Mason for tennis tournament (neighboring community)
~$1,000 – estimated cost to send staff to OTA winter conference
notable_topics: CPAAA memorial Christmas tree fundraiser proposal on township property
Zoning text amendment for temporary dwelling units during construction – voted down for revision
Finance committee testimony in Columbus for House Bill 244
New HR Director Melinda introduction and welcome
Revised authorized credit card users list
Fire department annual awards banquet October 21
Executive session – personnel matter (no decisions made)
attendees: Indy Evans (Chamber of Commerce President, mentioned)
notes: The zoning text amendment was deliberately voted down (not tabled) so it could be rewritten to include provisions for emergency situations (tornado, fire damage) allowing temporary dwelling units, in addition to new construction. The board discussed their recent trip to the Ohio State Capitol to testify before a finance committee regarding House Bill 244 requesting $2.5 million. Melinda, the new HR Director, was formally welcomed – she is a Goshen resident and graduate who recently returned to the area. The transcript contains significant repetition/audio artifacts near the end.
title: BOT 10-10-23
